svg.title: New pods arrive at a fraction of their capacity
svg.desc: A chart over time showing incoming load as a rising blue line and the pods that the horizontal pod autoscaler adds as a green step function beneath it. Because each newly started Java pod begins in the interpreter and needs time to reach its compiled peak, the effective capacity those pods deliver lags behind the step: the shaded red region between the pod count step and the effective capacity curve is the warmup deficit, the interval during which a pod is running and consuming its full resource request while delivering only a fraction of its throughput. Teams close that gap by keeping spare pods warm or by scaling out ahead of the traffic, and both of those are idle resources, which is to say both are money.
svg.text: the gap: pods are running and billed, but still warming up
